channel.title: Frontion News - Tier 1 Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: ⛪ Pope urges US and Iran to return to peace talks, condemns capital punishment
item.description: Pope Leo XIV called for de-escalation and a return to diplomatic negotiations between the US and Iran during his Africa tour, criticizing capital punishment while addressing the ongoing conflict. | Source: AP News | Category: Diplomacy

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump reclassifies medical marijuana as less-dangerous drug in historic shift
item.description: President Trump signed an order reclassifying state-licensed medical marijuana as a less-dangerous drug, marking a historic shift in US drug policy after decades of strict federal classification. | Source: AP News | Category: US Politics

item.title: 💰 Meta slashes 8,000 jobs as AI spending soars
item.description: Meta announced its largest layoff since 2023, cutting 10% of its workforce as the company ramps up AI infrastructure investments, joining other tech giants in cost-cutting measures. | Source: BBC | Category: Technology

item.title: 🌍 More than 500 killed in Tanzania election violence, inquiry finds
item.description: An official inquiry found over 500 people were killed during Tanzania elections, with opposition parties accusing security forces, though the report does not specify who was responsible for the deaths. | Source: BBC | Category: Africa

item.title: 🇨🇳 White House memo claims mass AI theft by Chinese firms
item.description: A White House memo from Michael Kratsios alleges that firms, mainly in China, are wrongfully distilling US AI models, raising tensions over intellectual property and technology competition. | Source: BBC | Category: US-China

item.title: 🇬🇧🇮🇱 UK Police Arrest Two More Over Alleged Iran-Backed Arson Plot Targeting Jewish Site
item.description: Police did not name a specific location but said the intended target was connected to the Jewish community. The arrests are part of a counter-terrorism investigation. | Source: Haaretz | Category: Middle East

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israeli Police Detain Professor Over Kippa With Israeli and Palestinian Flags, Cut Off Palestine Flag
item.description: Alex Sinclair says two officers detained him at a cafe, accusing him of incitement. When he refused to leave without his kippa, an officer cut out the Palestinian flag. | Source: Haaretz | Category: Middle East

item.title: 🏛️ Trump Administration Probes NYC Education Department Over Pro-Palestinian Group
item.description: The probe escalates federal pressure on local institutions over activities related to Israel-Gaza protests and speech. | Source: Al-Monitor | Category: US Politics

item.title: 🎨 Venice Biennale Jury Excludes Russia and Israel From Artist Awards as EU Threatens Funding Cut
item.description: The decision marks a cultural front opening in geopolitical tensions, with European Union officials questioning continued funding. | Source: Al-Monitor | Category: Diplomacy

item.title: 🌏 The Quad Is on the Brink of Extinction
item.description: The US-India-Japan-Australia security partnership faces existential questions as member states recalibrate their China strategies. | Source: Foreign Policy | Category: Asia-Pacific

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Orders Military to Shoot Iranian Boats Blocking Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump has authorized US forces to fire on Iranian small boats obstructing the strategic waterway, escalating tensions as the naval blockade continues. | Source: AP News | Category: US-Iran War

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Ceasefire by Three Weeks
item.description: Trump announced the extension after ambassadors from both countries met in Washington. The fragile truce was due to expire Sunday. | Source: BBC | Category: Middle East

item.title: 🛢️ US Boards Ship Carrying Iranian Oil as Blockade Intercepts 33 Vessels
item.description: US Central Command reports intercepting 33 vessels since imposing the blockade on ships traveling to and from Iran. | Source: BBC | Category: US-Iran War

item.title: ⚓ Navy Secretary John Phelan Leaves Post Effective Immediately
item.description: The Pentagon announced Phelan's departure amid tensions over US shipbuilding, the latest in a series of top defense leadership exits. | Source: AP News | Category: US Politics

item.title: 🇻🇪 US Soldier Charged After Betting $400,000 on Maduro's Removal Using Classified Info
item.description: Gannon Ken Van Dyke allegedly made trades on Polymarket based on classified information about the Venezuela operation. | Source: BBC | Category: Military

item.title: 🇬🇧🇺🇦 Prince Harry visits Kyiv in show of support for Ukraine
item.description: The Duke of Sussex attended a security conference in the Ukrainian capital, demonstrating continued Western support as global attention remains focused on the Middle East conflict. | Source: NBC News | Category: Ukraine-Russia War

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US boards Iranian oil tanker in Indian Ocean as blockade expands
item.description: US forces intercepted the M/T Majestic X carrying Iranian oil, marking an escalation of the naval blockade. Trump also ordered the Navy to "shoot and kill" any boats laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz. | Source: BBC | Category: US-Iran War

item.title: 🇵🇭⚖️ Philippines ex-president Duterte to face ICC trial for crimes against humanity
item.description: International Criminal Court judges ruled there are substantial grounds to believe Duterte guided the anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousands, clearing the way for a landmark trial. | Source: The Guardian | Category: Asia-Pacific

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳 Taiwan president blames China for cancellation of Africa trip
item.description: President Lai Ching-te postponed his visit to Eswatini after China pressured African countries to deny overflight permissions, in the latest sign of escalating cross-strait tensions. | Source: NBC News | Category: US-China

item.title: 🇦🇫🇺🇸 Trump officials consider sending 1,100 Afghan allies to Congo
item.description: Afghans who aided US forces during the 20-year war may be resettled in Congo instead of the United States, drawing criticism from veterans and advocacy groups. | Source: The Guardian | Category: US Politics

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦💰 EU Approves €106 Billion Ukraine Loan After Hungary Lifts Veto
item.description: The European Union formally approved a massive loan package to help Ukraine meet its economic and military needs for the next two years, overcoming Hungary's previous objections. | Source: SCMP | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇮🇷🇩🇪 Iran's Exiled Crown Prince Targeted with Red Substance in Berlin
item.description: Reza Pahlavi was splashed with red substance while leaving a press conference where he stated diplomacy with Iran 'has been given enough chance', amid ongoing Iran-US tensions. | Source: SCMP | Category: Middle East

item.title: 🇺🇸⚓ Trump's Navy Secretary Out After Reported Pentagon Clashes
item.description: John Phelan's abrupt departure comes as the US Navy enforces a high-stakes blockade of Iran, following the firing of a top US Army general. | Source: SCMP | Category: US Politics

item.title: 🇺🇸🇲🇽 Deaths of CIA Agents in Mexico Spark Sovereignty Row
item.description: President Sheinbaum spars with Washington over the role of Americans in a Chihuahua anti-drug operation, raising diplomatic tensions between the two countries. | Source: SCMP | Category: Americas

item.title: 🇺🇦🚁⚔️ Ukraine Unmanned Vessel Launches Interceptor Against Shahed Drone
item.description: Ukraine unveiled a powerful new defense against Shahed drones that could blunt Russia's ability to force concessions in future negotiations—the first such capability demonstrated. | Source: Defense News | Category: Defense Industry
